rejected_candidates.label: llm_sql_direct
rejected_candidates.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.doctor_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
WHERE p.patient_id IN (
    SELECT a2.patient_id
    FROM appointments a2
    JOIN doctors d2 ON a2.doctor_id = d2.doctor_id
    GROUP BY a2.patient_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT d2.specialty) > 1
)
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name
HAVING SUM(i.total_amount) > (
    SELECT AVG(total_amount)
    FROM invoices i2
    JOIN appointments a3 ON i2.appointment_id = a3.appointment_id
    JOIN patients p2 ON a3.patient_id = p2.patient_id
    WHERE p2.insurance_provider = p.insurance_provider
)
reasons: SQL uses unmentioned table 'invoices' while the question names specific tables ['doctors', 'patients']
grain violation: the comparison must use an aggregate of per-entity totals (AVG over SUM(total_amount) per patients.patient_id), but the SQL computes AVG(invoices.total_amount) directly over raw rows

semantic_checklist.comparison_logic: Patient's total invoiced amount must be greater than the average total amount of all patients sharing the same insurance provider

repair_triggers: selected candidate failed hard semantic checks

generated_sql.sql:
WITH patient_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        p.patient_id,
        p.patient_name,
        p.insurance_provider,
        SUM(i.total_amount) AS total_invoiced
    FROM patients p
    JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
    JOIN doctors d ON a.doctor_id = d.doctor_id
    JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
    GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT d.specialty) > 1
)
SELECT 
    pt.patient_id,
    pt.patient_name
FROM patient_totals pt
WHERE pt.total_invoiced > (
    SELECT AVG(pt2.total_invoiced)
    FROM patient_totals pt2
    WHERE pt2.insurance_provider = pt.insurance_provider
)

rejected_candidates.label: llm_sql_direct
rejected_candidates.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
WHERE i.payment_status IN ('unpaid', 'partial')
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
HAVING SUM(i.total_amount - i.insurance_paid) > (
    SELECT AVG(outstanding_balance)
    FROM (
        SELECT p2.insurance_provider, SUM(i2.total_amount - i2.insurance_paid) AS outstanding_balance
        FROM patients p2
        JOIN appointments a2 ON p2.patient_id = a2.patient_id
        JOIN invoices i2 ON a2.appointment_id = i2.appointment_id
        WHERE i2.payment_status IN ('unpaid', 'partial')
        GROUP BY p2.patient_id, p2.insurance_provider
    ) sub
    WHERE sub.insurance_provider = p.insurance_provider
)

semantic_checklist.comparison_logic: Compare each patient's unpaid balance to the average unpaid balance of patients with the same insurance provider

generated_sql.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
WHERE i.payment_status IN ('unpaid', 'partial')
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
HAVING SUM(i.total_amount - i.insurance_paid) > (
    SELECT AVG(sub.unpaid_balance)
    FROM (
        SELECT p2.patient_id, p2.insurance_provider, SUM(i2.total_amount - i2.insurance_paid) AS unpaid_balance
        FROM patients p2
        JOIN appointments a2 ON p2.patient_id = a2.patient_id
        JOIN invoices i2 ON a2.appointment_id = i2.appointment_id
        WHERE i2.payment_status IN ('unpaid', 'partial')
        GROUP BY p2.patient_id, p2.insurance_provider
    ) sub
    WHERE sub.insurance_provider = p.insurance_provider
)

)

rejected_candidates.sql:
WITH patient_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        p.patient_id,
        p.patient_name,
        p.insurance_provider,
        SUM(i.total_amount) AS lifetime_total
    FROM patients p
    JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
    JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
    WHERE a.status = 'completed'
    GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name, p.insurance_provider
),
recent_completed AS (
    SELECT 
        p.patient_id
    FROM patients p
    JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
    WHERE a.status = 'completed'
      AND a.appointment_date = (
          SELECT MAX(a2.appointment_date)
          FROM appointments a2
          WHERE a2.patient_id = p.patient_id
      )
)
SELECT 
    pt.patient_id,
    pt.patient_name
FROM patient_totals pt
JOIN recent_completed rc ON pt.patient_id = rc.patient_id
WHERE pt.lifetime_total > (
    SELECT AVG(pt2.lifetime_total)
    FROM patient_totals pt2
    WHERE pt2.insurance_provider = pt.insurance_provider
)

rejected_candidates.label: llm_sql_direct
rejected_candidates.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
JOIN lab_results l ON a.appointment_id = l.appointment_id
WHERE l.result_flag IN ('high', 'low', 'critical')
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.test_name) > 1
   AND (SUM(i.total_amount) - SUM(i.insurance_paid)) > (
       SELECT AVG(outstanding_balance)
       FROM (
           SELECT SUM(i2.total_amount) - SUM(i2.insurance_paid) AS outstanding_balance
           FROM appointments a2
           JOIN invoices i2 ON a2.appointment_id = i2.appointment_id
           GROUP BY a2.patient_id
       )
   )
reasons: SQL uses unmentioned table 'lab_results' while the question names specific tables ['patients']
SQL uses unmentioned table 'invoices' while the question names specific tables ['patients']

grain violation [requirement 2/2]: contract requires the measure over ALL rows of the entity (lifetime/overall SUM(invoices.total_amount) per patients.patient_id), but every SUM(total_amount) used in the comparison is computed from a restricted scope: measure scope is filtered by an unrelated qualifying condition on lab_results.result_flag
possible join fanout aggregate inflation

rejected_candidates.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
JOIN lab_results l ON a.appointment_id = l.appointment_id
WHERE l.result_flag IN ('high', 'low', 'critical')
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.test_name) > 1
   AND SUM(i.total_amount) - SUM(i.insurance_paid) > (
       SELECT AVG(total_outstanding)
       FROM (
           SELECT p2.patient_id, SUM(i2.total_amount) - SUM(i2.insurance_paid) AS total_outstanding
           FROM patients p2
           JOIN appointments a2 ON p2.patient_id = a2.patient_id
           JOIN invoices i2 ON a2.appointment_id = i2.appointment_id
           GROUP BY p2.patient_id
       )
   )

rejected_candidates.label: llm_sql_direct_variant
rejected_candidates.sql:
SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_name
FROM patients p
JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
JOIN lab_results l ON a.appointment_id = l.appointment_id
WHERE l.result_flag IN ('high', 'low', 'critical')
GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.test_name) > 1

       SELECT AVG(total_amount - insurance_paid)

semantic_checklist.comparison_logic: Compare patient's outstanding balance to the average outstanding balance of all patients

repair_triggers: selected SQL uses literals not seen in the sampled data

generated_sql.sql:
WITH patient_abnormal_tests AS (
    SELECT 
        p.patient_id,
        p.patient_name,
        COUNT(DISTINCT lr.test_name) AS abnormal_test_count
    FROM patients p
    JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
    JOIN lab_results lr ON a.appointment_id = lr.appointment_id
    WHERE lr.result_flag IN ('high', 'low', 'critical')
    GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.patient_name
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT lr.test_name) > 1
),
patient_balances AS (
    SELECT 
        p.patient_id,
        SUM(i.total_amount) - SUM(i.insurance_paid) AS outstanding_balance
    FROM patients p
    JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id
    JOIN invoices i ON a.appointment_id = i.appointment_id
    GROUP BY p.patient_id
),
avg_balance AS (
    SELECT AVG(outstanding_balance) AS avg_outstanding
    FROM patient_balances
)
SELECT 
    pat.patient_id,
    pat.patient_name
FROM patient_abnormal_tests pat
JOIN patient_balances pb ON pat.patient_id = pb.patient_id
CROSS JOIN avg_balance ab
WHERE pb.outstanding_balance > ab.avg_outstanding
